markets.question: New Rihanna Album before GTA VI?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Rihanna officially releases a new album before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date.

Re-releases, deluxe editions, compilations, or greatest hits albums will not count, unless they contain at least 50% tracks that have never been officially released in any format prior to the album's release.

The resolution source for the album release will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify.

events.title: What will happen before GTA VI?
events.description: This is a market on predicting events that will occur before the release of Grand Theft Auto VI.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rockstar Games and parent company Take-Two have locked Grand Theft Auto VI for a November 19, 2026 release following multiple delays from the original fall 2025 window and a brief May 2026 target. This extended timeline through late 2026 creates a wide window for unrelated events across geopolitics, music, technology, and finance to unfold beforehand, shaping trader focus on outcomes with clear resolution criteria like official ceasefires or album drops. Recent earnings commentary from Take-Two has reinforced the date without signaling further shifts, while industry attention turns to potential marketing updates or earnings calls that could influence perception of the game's readiness. The market aggregates skin-in-the-game views on what crosses the finish line first amid the long runway.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rockstar Games’ official November 2025 announcement pushed Grand Theft Auto VI from its prior May 26, 2026 target to November 19, 2026, establishing the clearest driver behind the market’s 100% consensus on no release before June. With the original window now behind us and no credible reports of an accelerated schedule, traders see virtually no room for an upset; the next verifiable milestone remains the confirmed fall date itself.

markets.question: Will Harvey Weinstein be sentenced to no prison time?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Harvey Weinstein as part of his ongoing New York retrial (People of the State of New York v. Harvey Weinstein) by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Weinstein is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

If the sentence falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent developments, including the May 15 mistrial in Harvey Weinstein’s third New York rape trial after a deadlocked jury on the Jessica Mann charge, have reinforced trader consensus around limited additional incarceration. Prosecutors now weigh a potential fourth trial ahead of a June 24 hearing, while Weinstein, age 74 and dealing with serious health issues, continues serving a 16-year California sentence and faces pending New York sentencing on a separate count that could add substantial time. The market-implied 64.1% probability on no further prison time reflects skepticism about new convictions or consecutive terms given repeated hung juries, his advanced age, appeals in progress, and the cumulative effect of existing sentences across jurisdictions.

markets.question: Will the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup based off the rules of the NHL.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NHL.

events.title: 2026 NHL Stanley Cup Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NHL Stanley Cup championship.

eventMetadata.context_description: The 2026 Stanley Cup Final pits the Carolina Hurricanes against the Vegas Golden Knights, with Game 1 set for June 2 in Raleigh. Carolina enters as the clear favorite after capturing the Eastern Conference title with a five-game series win over the Montreal Canadiens, including a decisive 6-1 Game 5 victory, marking their first Final appearance since 2006. Their regular-season dominance and home-ice advantage in a best-of-seven series underpin the 58.5% implied probability. Vegas reached the Final by sweeping the Presidents' Trophy-winning Colorado Avalanche in the Western Conference, fueled by a strong run since John Tortorella's midseason arrival as coach. The Golden Knights' playoff resilience and experience support their 41.4% positioning in this closely contested matchup.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NBA.

events.title: 2026 NBA Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NBA Finals.

eventMetadata.context_description: The San Antonio Spurs hold a clear edge in the 2026 NBA champion market due to their Western Conference Finals triumph over the Oklahoma City Thunder, capped by a Game 7 victory that propelled them into the NBA Finals against the New York Knicks. Victor Wembanyama's dominance and the Spurs' 62-20 regular-season mark have fueled strong recent form and roster continuity, positioning them as series favorites with implied probabilities aligning to roughly two-thirds chance of claiming the title. The Knicks, who swept the Cleveland Cavaliers to secure the East, bring momentum from an 11-game win streak and Jalen Brunson's leadership, yet face a tougher road matchup against a deeper Western contender. Trader consensus reflects these head-to-head dynamics and playoff trends more than historical regular-season standings.

markets.question: Will MegaETH perform an airdrop by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if MegaETH launches a token and performs an airdrop by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market "locked" tokens or non-swappable tokens will not suffice to resolve this market to "Yes". Airdrops of NFTs will not qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market is on-chain information and official information from MegaETH, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

markets.question: Will Spain win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: The closely bunched probabilities among France, Spain, England, Portugal, Brazil, and Argentina reflect deep squad talent, strong recent international results, and the uncertainties of an expanded 48-team field set to begin in June. France and Spain sit atop the implied probabilities after consistent performances in qualifiers and friendlies, while England benefits from depth in midfield and attack. Brazil and Argentina carry pedigree from recent Copa America and World Cup success, yet face tougher paths through the group stage. Portugal's attacking options keep them competitive despite lacking a prior title. This parity among top contenders, combined with potential for upsets in a larger tournament, sustains the tight trader consensus across the board.

markets.question: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gavin Newsom leads the wide-open 2028 Democratic presidential nomination market at 23.8% amid an early field shaped by name recognition and positioning after the 2024 general election loss. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez at 9.0% and Kamala Harris at 7.8% reflect progressive and recent general-election support, respectively, while governors and senators such as Pete Buttigieg and Josh Shapiro draw moderate interest in hypothetical polls. Trader consensus highlights the lack of a clear frontrunner, with probabilities sensitive to future polling, fundraising, and endorsements.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gavin Newsom leads the wide-open 2028 Democratic presidential nomination market at 23.8% amid an early field shaped by name recognition and positioning after the 2024 general election loss. As term-limited California governor, he has pursued national visibility through public responses to the Trump administration, donor engagement, and recent policy moves on issues like AI regulation. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez at 9.0% and Kamala Harris at 7.8% reflect progressive and recent general-election support, respectively, while governors and senators such as Pete Buttigieg and Josh Shapiro draw moderate interest in hypothetical polls. Recent developments include AOC’s national tour and outreach to early states, Whitmer’s decision against running, and prospective candidates’ focus on Southern voters ahead of the 2026 midterms that will influence primary timing and consolidation. Trader consensus highlights the lack of a clear frontrunner, with probabilities sensitive to future polling, fundraising, and endorsements.
